mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in downturn periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing starts rate above 1.2 million signals a surge in housing demand that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in boom periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 99 signals a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profits and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in disruption periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a labor market upturn that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in upturn periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real GDP growth rate above 3.5% signals a surge in demand that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 95 signals a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profits and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in disruption periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
